note: SEO Checklist is a Checklist API consumer: it registers one checklist via hook_checklistapi_checklist_info() and provides no config form, permissions, services, or Drush of its own. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the SEO Checklist module actually provide, and which module does it depend on to work?
evals.expected_output: A persistent, fillable checklist of SEO tasks/recommended modules; it provides little on its own and depends on Checklist API (checklistapi:checklistapi).

evals.prompt: Where do I open the SEO Checklist in the admin UI, and what is its configure route name?
evals.expected_output: At /admin/config/search/seo-checklist (Admin > Configuration > Search and metadata > SEO Checklist); the configure route is checklistapi.checklists.seo_checklist, provided by Checklist API.

evals.prompt: How does the SEO Checklist module define its list of SEO tasks in code? Which hook does it implement?
evals.expected_output: It implements hook_checklistapi_checklist_info() (seo_checklist_checklistapi_checklist_info) to register the 'seo_checklist' checklist, with a #callback returning the grouped items.

evals.prompt: In the SEO Checklist item definitions, which custom key marks a recommended contrib module (and pre-checks the item if it is installed), and which key points to that module's configuration route?
evals.expected_output: #module is the recommended module's project/machine name (pre-checks via #default_value when installed and adds Download/Install links); #configure is the route name of that module's settings page, rendered as a Configure link.

evals.prompt: Where does the SEO Checklist store which items have been completed, and what happens to that data when the module is uninstalled?
evals.expected_output: Progress is stored by Checklist API in the config object checklistapi.progress.seo_checklist; hook_uninstall() deletes it when the module is uninstalled.

evals.prompt: From a custom module I want to add an extra task to the SEO Checklist. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_checklistapi_checklist_info_alter() (provided by Checklist API) and modify $definitions['seo_checklist'].

evals.prompt: Does the SEO Checklist module define its own permissions, config settings form, or Drush commands?
evals.expected_output: No — it defines no permissions, no settings form, no services, and no Drush commands of its own; the route, permissions, UI, and persistence come from Checklist API.
